references.title: Predicting course achievement of university students based on their procrastination behaviour on Moodle

extracted.Student Performance Definition: End of Course Grade - Inferred from Clustering
extracted.Students: 242 university students from University of Tartu
extracted.Features: [For each Homework, OpenD, FirstviewD, SubmissionD, Deadline, S]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
For each Homework, the authors extract: when it is open, viewed and submitte. This allows the creation of the feature vector X which includes measurements of time. 

These feature vectors are subsequently given to a clustering algorithm to obtain a label. The authors use different methods depending on the fact that the input feature vectors are categorical or continuous.
extracted.Models: L-SVM, R-SVM, Gaussian Process, Decision Tree, Random Forest, MLP, AdaBoost, Naive Bayes
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Precision: 0.870 - L-SVM_10folds_Cat, Accuracy: 0.846 - L-SVM_5folds_Cat, F1-Score: 0.857 - L-SVM_15folds_Cont, Cohen’s Kappa: 0.771 - L_SVM_15folds_Cat}
extracted.Comments:
Authors create 2 distinct feature sets: one continuous and one categorical and check the performance of both.

All variables are indicative of procrastination behavior. 

Different k are tested for Cross-Validation.
extracted.Limitations:
Single institution, 

Only considers homework submission data

No “early prediction”

Target is not actual ground truth… it is inferred from Clustering

extracted.Students: 242 university students from University of Tartu
extracted.Features: [For each Homework, OpenD, FirstviewD, SubmissionD, Deadline, S, H]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
For each Homework, the authors extract: when it is open, viewed and submitte. This allows the creation of the feature vector X which includes measurements of time. 

The authors use different methods depending on the fact that the input feature vectors are categorical or continuous.
extracted.Cluster Description: C1: A, C2: B, C3: C, C4: D, C5: E, C6: F
extracted.Implications:
There is a noticeable correlation between procrastinating behaviors and the performance on the course. 

Students who procrastinate more tend to do worse in the course.
extracted.Comments: Authors use clustering to identify students based on their grade. Clustering, in this instance is made to estimate what the final course grade would be based on the homework submissions.
extracted.Limitations:
Single institution, 

Only considers homework submission data

annotations.text: Procrastination behavior is a negatively affects student performance. Work intends to measure procrastination behavior based on Moodle submission Logs.

Authors start by creating vectors of student procrastination tendencies. Students are then grouped using clustering and classification techniques in order to be assigned to different categories of course achievement. Results show that their model achieves 87% precision and 84% accuracy on a sample of 242 students.
Students who procrastinate more are more likely to do badly in the course.
